content.content:
1	14/P02 — HEADER AND PRIMARY NAV
2	==================================
3	
4	GOAL
5	----
6	A compact, always-visible header carrying the site identity and
7	five nav links. Per principle 5: present on every page.
8	
9	§A FILES TOUCHED
10	----------------
11	  src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx    (replace stub from P01)
12	  src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx       (NEW — knows about active state)
13	  src/components/shell/SkipLink.tsx      (NEW)
14	
15	§B ACCEPTANCE
16	-------------
17	B1.  Layout: full-width strip, height 56px (desktop) / 52px (mobile).
18	     Uses Container size="wide" inside. Border-bottom: 1px var(--line).
19	     Background: var(--bg) (matches page bg, no separate header bg).
20	
21	B2.  Left side: site identity. Mono text, var(--t-md-size), weight 600:
22	       "michael quintin"   (lowercase intentional, mono aesthetic)
23	     Wraps in <Link href="/" variant="subtle"> — clicking returns
24	     to landing. NOT a logo image.
25	
26	B3.  Right side (desktop, >=768px): horizontal nav with five items
27	     using NavLink. Each NavLink:
28	       Props: href, label
29	       Active state: when pathname starts with href (or === "/" when
30	       label === "Home"), the link's text color is var(--fg) and
31	       text-decoration is var(--accent) underline 2px. Inactive
32	       state: text color var(--fg-mute), no underline.
33	       Hover: text color var(--fg), underline appears in --line-strong.
34	       Transitions on color/text-decoration over var(--d-fast).
35	
36	B4.  Right side (mobile, <768px): a hamburger button that opens the
37	     mobile drawer (P05 implements the drawer; P02 includes the
38	     button stub that toggles a state lifted to a context or zustand
39	     slice; pick whatever the project uses for shared state. If
40	     none exists, use React context).
41	
42	B5.  SkipLink renders before everything else, visually-hidden until
43	     focused; on focus it appears as a button at the top-left
44	     reading "Skip to content". Pressing Enter focuses #main.
45	
46	B6.  ARIA: header gets role="banner". The nav gets aria-label="Primary".
47	     Active nav link gets aria-current="page".
48	
49	§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
50	------------------------
51	  test -f src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx
52	  test -f src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx
53	  test -f src/components/shell/SkipLink.tsx
54	  # active state: check that NavLink reads usePathname
55	  grep -n 'usePathname' src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx
56	
57	§D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO
58	------------------------
59	  • Do NOT add a search box. /chat IS the search interface.
60	  • Do NOT make the header sticky on mobile (creates iOS Safari
61	    keyboard issues with /chat).
62	  • Do NOT animate the nav-link active underline; it appears
63	    instantly on route change.
64	
65	§E REPORT
66	---------
67	IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL
68

file.content:
14/P02 — HEADER AND PRIMARY NAV
==================================

GOAL
----
A compact, always-visible header carrying the site identity and
five nav links. Per principle 5: present on every page.

§A FILES TOUCHED
----------------
  src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx    (replace stub from P01)
  src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx       (NEW — knows about active state)
  src/components/shell/SkipLink.tsx      (NEW)

§B ACCEPTANCE
-------------
B1.  Layout: full-width strip, height 56px (desktop) / 52px (mobile).
     Uses Container size="wide" inside. Border-bottom: 1px var(--line).
     Background: var(--bg) (matches page bg, no separate header bg).

B2.  Left side: site identity. Mono text, var(--t-md-size), weight 600:
       "michael quintin"   (lowercase intentional, mono aesthetic)
     Wraps in <Link href="/" variant="subtle"> — clicking returns
     to landing. NOT a logo image.

B3.  Right side (desktop, >=768px): horizontal nav with five items
     using NavLink. Each NavLink:
       Props: href, label
       Active state: when pathname starts with href (or === "/" when
       label === "Home"), the link's text color is var(--fg) and
       text-decoration is var(--accent) underline 2px. Inactive
       state: text color var(--fg-mute), no underline.
       Hover: text color var(--fg), underline appears in --line-strong.
       Transitions on color/text-decoration over var(--d-fast).

B4.  Right side (mobile, <768px): a hamburger button that opens the
     mobile drawer (P05 implements the drawer; P02 includes the
     button stub that toggles a state lifted to a context or zustand
     slice; pick whatever the project uses for shared state. If
     none exists, use React context).

B5.  SkipLink renders before everything else, visually-hidden until
     focused; on focus it appears as a button at the top-left
     reading "Skip to content". Pressing Enter focuses #main.

B6.  ARIA: header gets role="banner". The nav gets aria-label="Primary".
     Active nav link gets aria-current="page".

§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
------------------------
  test -f src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx
  test -f src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx
  test -f src/components/shell/SkipLink.tsx
  # active state: check that NavLink reads usePathname
  grep -n 'usePathname' src/components/shell/NavLink.tsx

§D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO
------------------------
  • Do NOT add a search box. /chat IS the search interface.
  • Do NOT make the header sticky on mobile (creates iOS Safari
    keyboard issues with /chat).
  • Do NOT animate the nav-link active underline; it appears
    instantly on route change.

§E REPORT
---------
IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL

1 14/P04 — ROUTING STRUCTURE AND PROJECTS INDEX 2 ================================================ 3 4 GOAL 5 ---- 6 Define the v2 route tree. Replace lingering v1 routes. Build the 7 /projects index page (a simple list/grid linking to per-project pages). 8 9 §A FILES TOUCHED 10 ---------------- 11 src/app/page.tsx (stays a stub for now; suite 15 owns landing) 12 src/app/projects/page.tsx (NEW — index) 13 src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx (rewrite — replaced fully in suite 16; here just a stub) 14 src/app/about/page.tsx (stub for now; suite 16 owns) 15 src/app/resume/page.tsx (stub; suite 16 owns) 16 src/app/chat/page.tsx (NEW; suite 19 fills) 17 src/app/hivemind/page.tsx (DELETE; redirects via next.config.ts to /projects/hivemind) 18 src/app/purposeless-efficiency/page.tsx (DELETE; redirect) 19 src/app/theseus/page.tsx (DELETE; redirect) 20 next.config.ts (add redirects) 21 src/content/projects/index.ts (NEW — projects metadata for the index page) 22 23 §B ACCEPTANCE 24 ------------- 25 B1. src/content/projects/index.ts exports: 26 export const projects = [ 27 { slug: "hivemind", code: "HVM", title: "Hivemind", 28 tagline: "", 29 status: "in-progress" | "shipped" | "exploration" }, 30 { slug: "purposeless-efficiency", code: "PRP", title: "Purposeless Efficiency", 31 tagline: "...", status: "..." }, 32 { slug: "theseus", code: "THS", title: "Theseus", 33 tagline: "...", status: "..." }, 34 ]; 35 Tagline is sourced from existing repo content 36 (src/content/projects//thesis.ts, etc.) and capped at 80 37 chars. If a tagline cannot be sourced from existing content, 38 ask the implementer to flag and skip rather than invent. 39 40 B2. /projects/page.tsx renders Container size="base" with: 41

Projects

42 Stack of three Card components, one per project. Each Card 43 href={"/projects/" + slug}, title=title, meta={code + " · " + status}, 44 children=tagline. 45 46 B3. Per-project routes /projects/[slug]/page.tsx — stubbed to 47 render for now. Suite 16/P04-P06 fills. 48 49 B4. Old slug-direct routes /hivemind, /theseus, 50 /purposeless-efficiency are removed and replaced by Next.js 51 redirects in next.config.ts: 52 redirects: async () => [ 53 { source: "/hivemind", destination: "/projects/hivemind", permanent: true }, 54 { source: "/purposeless-efficiency", destination: "/projects/purposeless-efficiency", permanent: true }, 55 { source: "/theseus", destination: "/projects/theseus", permanent: true }, 56 ] 57 Permanent (308) so SEO carries over. 58 59 B5. /chat/page.tsx is a stub that renders for 60 now. Suite 19 implements. 61 62 B6. generateStaticParams in /projects/[slug]/page.tsx returns the 63 three slugs from src/content/projects/index.ts. Unknown slugs 64 trigger notFound(). 65 66 §C VERIFICATION COMMANDS 67 ------------------------ 68 test -f src/app/projects/page.tsx 69 test -f src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx 70 test -f src/app/chat/page.tsx 71 test -f src/content/projects/index.ts 72 ! test -d src/app/hivemind || (echo "FAIL"; false) 73 ! test -d src/app/theseus || (echo "FAIL"; false) 74 ! test -d src/app/purposeless-efficiency || (echo "FAIL"; false) 75 grep -n 'redirects' next.config.ts 76 npm run build 77 78 §D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO 79 ------------------------ 80 • Do NOT remove src/lib/panels.ts. The slug→title mapping is 81 consumed elsewhere. 82 • Do NOT add /resume or /about as redirects; they are real pages. 83 84 §E REPORT 85 --------- 86 IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL 87

14/P04 — ROUTING STRUCTURE AND PROJECTS INDEX ================================================ GOAL ---- Define the v2 route tree. Replace lingering v1 routes. Build the /projects index page (a simple list/grid linking to per-project pages). §A FILES TOUCHED ---------------- src/app/page.tsx (stays a stub for now; suite 15 owns landing) src/app/projects/page.tsx (NEW — index) src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx (rewrite — replaced fully in suite 16; here just a stub) src/app/about/page.tsx (stub for now; suite 16 owns) src/app/resume/page.tsx (stub; suite 16 owns) src/app/chat/page.tsx (NEW; suite 19 fills) src/app/hivemind/page.tsx (DELETE; redirects via next.config.ts to /projects/hivemind) src/app/purposeless-efficiency/page.tsx (DELETE; redirect) src/app/theseus/page.tsx (DELETE; redirect) next.config.ts (add redirects) src/content/projects/index.ts (NEW — projects metadata for the index page) §B ACCEPTANCE ------------- B1. src/content/projects/index.ts exports: export const projects = [ { slug: "hivemind", code: "HVM", title: "Hivemind", tagline: "", status: "in-progress" | "shipped" | "exploration" }, { slug: "purposeless-efficiency", code: "PRP", title: "Purposeless Efficiency", tagline: "...", status: "..." }, { slug: "theseus", code: "THS", title: "Theseus", tagline: "...", status: "..." }, ]; Tagline is sourced from existing repo content (src/content/projects//thesis.ts, etc.) and capped at 80 chars. If a tagline cannot be sourced from existing content, ask the implementer to flag and skip rather than invent. B2. /projects/page.tsx renders Container size="base" with:

Projects

Stack of three Card components, one per project. Each Card href={"/projects/" + slug}, title=title, meta={code + " · " + status}, children=tagline. B3. Per-project routes /projects/[slug]/page.tsx — stubbed to render for now. Suite 16/P04-P06 fills. B4. Old slug-direct routes /hivemind, /theseus, /purposeless-efficiency are removed and replaced by Next.js redirects in next.config.ts: redirects: async () => [ { source: "/hivemind", destination: "/projects/hivemind", permanent: true }, { source: "/purposeless-efficiency", destination: "/projects/purposeless-efficiency", permanent: true }, { source: "/theseus", destination: "/projects/theseus", permanent: true }, ] Permanent (308) so SEO carries over. B5. /chat/page.tsx is a stub that renders for now. Suite 19 implements. B6. generateStaticParams in /projects/[slug]/page.tsx returns the three slugs from src/content/projects/index.ts. Unknown slugs trigger notFound(). §C VERIFICATION COMMANDS ------------------------ test -f src/app/projects/page.tsx test -f src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx test -f src/app/chat/page.tsx test -f src/content/projects/index.ts ! test -d src/app/hivemind || (echo "FAIL"; false) ! test -d src/app/theseus || (echo "FAIL"; false) ! test -d src/app/purposeless-efficiency || (echo "FAIL"; false) grep -n 'redirects' next.config.ts npm run build §D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO ------------------------ • Do NOT remove src/lib/panels.ts. The slug→title mapping is consumed elsewhere. • Do NOT add /resume or /about as redirects; they are real pages. §E REPORT --------- IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL

content.content:
1	14/P05 — MOBILE DRAWER NAV
2	=============================
3	
4	GOAL
5	----
6	A simple slide-in drawer for the mobile nav (<768px). Hamburger
7	in the header (P02) toggles it. Per principle 11: same nav items,
8	same content access; layout flexes.
9	
10	§A FILES TOUCHED
11	----------------
12	  src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx              (NEW)
13	  src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx                (modify: wire up the toggle)
14	  src/components/shell/useNavToggle.tsx              (NEW — context for open/close state)
15	
16	§B ACCEPTANCE
17	-------------
18	B1.  useNavToggle is a tiny context: { open: boolean, toggle: () => void, close: () => void }.
19	     Provider lives at the root of the layout (so SiteHeader and
20	     MobileDrawer can both read/write).
21	
22	B2.  MobileDrawer renders only when isNarrow. Implementation:
23	       - <div role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Navigation"
24	              data-open={open}>
25	       - data-open=true sets transform: translateX(0); data-open=false
26	         sets transform: translateX(100%). Transition: var(--d-norm)
27	         var(--ease-out).
28	       - Backdrop: position fixed, inset 0, bg var(--bg-overlay),
29	         pointer-events when open. Click to close.
30	       - Drawer panel: width min(320px, 80vw), height 100dvh, bg
31	         var(--bg-mute), padding var(--s-6).
32	       - Inside: a vertical list of NavLinks. Same five items as
33	         desktop. Active state styled identically.
34	       - Close button (X) at top-right, mono, aria-label="Close
35	         navigation".
36	
37	B3.  Body scroll lock when open. Apply overflow: hidden to <body>
38	     when drawer is open. Remove on close.
39	
40	B4.  Focus trap: when open, focus moves to the close button. Tab
41	     cycles within the drawer. Escape closes. The trap library is
42	     not required; a small useEffect that listens for Tab and
43	     redirects focus is acceptable.
44	
45	B5.  Closes on route change. Use Next's usePathname; useEffect that
46	     calls close() on pathname change.
47	
48	B6.  Reduced-motion: drawer uses opacity instead of transform
49	     transition.
50	
51	§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
52	------------------------
53	  test -f src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx
54	  test -f src/components/shell/useNavToggle.tsx
55	  grep -n 'aria-modal="true"' src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx
56	  grep -n 'usePathname' src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx
57	
58	§D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO
59	------------------------
60	  • Do NOT use a third-party drawer/dialog library.
61	  • Do NOT add gesture-based open/close (swipe-from-edge). Tap-only.
62	  • Do NOT animate the hamburger icon morphing into an X. Plain
63	    toggle.
64	
65	§E REPORT
66	---------
67	IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL
68

file.content:
14/P05 — MOBILE DRAWER NAV
=============================

GOAL
----
A simple slide-in drawer for the mobile nav (<768px). Hamburger
in the header (P02) toggles it. Per principle 11: same nav items,
same content access; layout flexes.

§A FILES TOUCHED
----------------
  src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx              (NEW)
  src/components/shell/SiteHeader.tsx                (modify: wire up the toggle)
  src/components/shell/useNavToggle.tsx              (NEW — context for open/close state)

§B ACCEPTANCE
-------------
B1.  useNavToggle is a tiny context: { open: boolean, toggle: () => void, close: () => void }.
     Provider lives at the root of the layout (so SiteHeader and
     MobileDrawer can both read/write).

B2.  MobileDrawer renders only when isNarrow. Implementation:
       - <div role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Navigation"
              data-open={open}>
       - data-open=true sets transform: translateX(0); data-open=false
         sets transform: translateX(100%). Transition: var(--d-norm)
         var(--ease-out).
       - Backdrop: position fixed, inset 0, bg var(--bg-overlay),
         pointer-events when open. Click to close.
       - Drawer panel: width min(320px, 80vw), height 100dvh, bg
         var(--bg-mute), padding var(--s-6).
       - Inside: a vertical list of NavLinks. Same five items as
         desktop. Active state styled identically.
       - Close button (X) at top-right, mono, aria-label="Close
         navigation".

B3.  Body scroll lock when open. Apply overflow: hidden to <body>
     when drawer is open. Remove on close.

B4.  Focus trap: when open, focus moves to the close button. Tab
     cycles within the drawer. Escape closes. The trap library is
     not required; a small useEffect that listens for Tab and
     redirects focus is acceptable.

B5.  Closes on route change. Use Next's usePathname; useEffect that
     calls close() on pathname change.

B6.  Reduced-motion: drawer uses opacity instead of transform
     transition.

§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
------------------------
  test -f src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx
  test -f src/components/shell/useNavToggle.tsx
  grep -n 'aria-modal="true"' src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx
  grep -n 'usePathname' src/components/shell/MobileDrawer.tsx

§D NON-GOALS / DO NOT DO
------------------------
  • Do NOT use a third-party drawer/dialog library.
  • Do NOT add gesture-based open/close (swipe-from-edge). Tap-only.
  • Do NOT animate the hamburger icon morphing into an X. Plain
    toggle.

§E REPORT
---------

content.text:
All acceptance criteria met:

- `SiteHeader.tsx`, `NavLink.tsx`, `SkipLink.tsx` all created
- `NavLink.tsx` reads `usePathname` (lines 4, 13)
- Header layout: 56px desktop / 52px mobile, `Container size="wide"`, `border-bottom: 1px var(--line)`, `background: var(--bg)`
- Identity "michael quintin" — mono, var(--t-md-size), weight 600, wrapped in `<Link href="/" variant="subtle">`
- Desktop nav (≥768px): five `NavLink`s — Home, Projects, About, Resume, Chat
- Active state via `pathname === "/"` for Home, `pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/")` for others; `aria-current="page"` + accent underline 2px, fg color
- Inactive: var(--fg-mute), no underline; hover: var(--fg) + var(--line-strong) underline
- Transitions on `color`/`text-decoration-color` over var(--d-fast); active state uses `transition: none` (no animated underline on route change)
- Mobile (<768px): hamburger button toggles local state (lifted within the client component; P05 will swap to its own `useNavToggle` context)
- SkipLink: fixed top-left, hidden via translate, revealed on focus; Enter focuses `#main`
- ARIA: `role="banner"` on header, `aria-label="Primary"` on nav, `aria-current="page"` on active link

Verification:
- `npm run typecheck` — clean
- `npm run lint` — only pre-existing warnings in `_legacy_v1/`
- `npm test` — 33/33 pass
- `npm run build` — succeeds

IMPL_RESULT: PASS
